additional_prompt: Please only output the 'covered' scenarios in one similarly structured json file. Also generate the customer_data which will be asked for in an extra field for each scenario (e.g. device: 'iPhone 15 pro' or 'iPhone 16 (256 GB)', plan agreement number: 'XXXXX' or 'Unknown', serial number: 'XXXXX', phone number: 'XXXXX', email: 'XXXXX').  The rest of the json should remain unchanged. Remember that part of this information (the title, description, and customer_data) will be used as basis for a synthetic conversation, thus it will need to e.g. a specific number as serial number that mimics the actual format.

covered.title: Hardware defect — microphone stops working
covered.description: Within 12 months, the iPhone’s microphone fails with no accidental damage.

service_fee.notes: No ADH fee for hardware defects

policy_references.snippet: a defect in materials and workmanship has arisen... Apple will either (a) repair... or (b) exchange...

covered.reasoning: Defect in materials/workmanship during Plan Term is explicitly covered.
required_user_inputs: Plan Agreement Number or serial number (Sec. 5)

covered.title: Battery capacity depleted ≥50%
covered.description: After 18 months, battery health reports ~40% of original capacity; device dies quickly.

policy_references.snippet: battery... capacity... has depleted fifty (50%) percent or more from its original specifications

covered.reasoning: Battery depletion ≥50% triggers covered repair or replacement.

covered.title: Accidental drop — cracked screen
covered.description: Phone slips from hand onto tile; display shatters and touch stops responding.

service_fee.notes: CAD$49 in Canada (Sec. 12.1). Plus applicable tax.

policy_references.snippet: accidental damage from handling… (e.g., drops and liquid contact)
You will pay a $49 (U.S.) dollar service fee… for each ADH claim
as a result of ADH claims… two (2) Service Events

covered.reasoning: A drop is an unexpected external event from normal daily usage; covered with ADH fee and counts toward the two-event limit.

covered.title: Liquid spill — phone won’t power on
covered.description: Coffee spills onto the iPhone during normal use at a desk; device fails.
covered.reasoning: Liquid contact from an unexpected event qualifies as ADH.

covered.title: Fall down stairs — enclosure bent, phone dead
covered.description: Phone slips from pocket, tumbles down stairs, no power afterwards.

policy_references.snippet: operational or mechanical failure caused by an accident from handling… unexpected and unintentional external event

covered.reasoning: Unintentional drop during normal daily usage is covered as ADH.

covered.title: Second ADH event within term
covered.description: After one prior ADH screen repair, device is later dropped into a sink and fails.
covered.reasoning: Second ADH claim is still covered; it exhausts ADH coverage for the Plan Term.

covered.title: Defective Face ID module
covered.description: Face ID stops working without any accidental damage or third-party repair.

policy_references.snippet: defect in materials and workmanship… repair… or exchange

covered.reasoning: Non-accidental functional failure is covered under hardware service.

covered.title: Charging port fails intermittently (no damage)
covered.description: Phone stops charging unless cable is held at an angle; no signs of abuse.

policy_references.snippet: defect in materials and workmanship… repair or exchange

covered.reasoning: Functional defect without accidental cause falls under 3.1.
